title: Other Scientific and Technical Consulting Services

narrative.executive_view: 541690 is a heterogeneous residual code, not a clean consulting vertical. The investable $1–10M EBITDA slice is most plausibly specialist technical advisory with repeat compliance, energy, safety, or field-linked work, rather than generic analysis alone. AI can remove meaningful documentation and analytical labor, but owner-expert concentration, scope uncertainty, and hourly billing make the value migration less clean than the high labor share suggests [S1][S5].
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI is most useful in desk work: research synthesis, first drafts of reports and proposals, data cleaning, benchmarking, meeting notes, and routine project administration. It is less substitutive for site inspection, sampling, testimony, accountable professional judgment, and the relationship work that wins specialist referrals. The 4-digit 5416 occupation mix is an imperfect proxy for this catch-all; the residual is deliberately treated as only partly automatable rather than assuming all unlisted specialists are analysts [S1][S2].
narrative.value_capture: The dominant advisory hypothesis is a mix of project and hourly expert work, with a smaller share of recurring compliance, seasonal, or shared-savings engagements. Faster production initially raises margin, but competitive bids and client rescoping can pass much of the saving through when hours are the billing unit. Credentials, liability, proprietary local data, and physical inspection protect selected niches, while a named expert relationship is portable and weakens firm-level moat [S5].
narrative.adoption_timing: Census measured AI use at 17%–20% across U.S. businesses from December 2025 through May 2026, with lower use among the smallest firms [S2]. This is a broad, all-function adoption anchor rather than a 541690 result. A five-year estimate to 50% effective adoption reflects tool availability alongside fragmented owner-operated firms, client confidentiality, validation needs, and physical/credentialed delivery; it does not rely on consolidation or owner age.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic dataset identifies 1,139 firms in the modeled EBITDA band, but the code’s large sub-five-employee population means many apparent targets are SDE/key-person practices. Equiteq reports continued buyer appetite for specialist consulting assets with deep expertise, analytics, and scalable delivery, while its report is global and not a 541690 transaction-comps file [S5]. Buyability is therefore real but conditional on transferable clients, second-line talent, and normalized owner compensation. The selected 5.0x buy and 5.5x exit EBITDA anchors are conservative estimates between micro-business cash-flow evidence and specialty-consulting strategic appetite, not observed 541690 comps [S3][S5].
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand is PRESSURED rather than melting: a client can increasingly obtain generic analytical output, and AI shortens the billable analytical portion of a project. Demand remains for work whose output must be defended to a regulator, insurer, court, or operating team, and for field observation that a model cannot perform. This is a terminal-demand mechanism distinct from the price-retention estimate; it does not itself increase C.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest uncertainty is market composition: NAICS 541690 spans economic, energy, agricultural, safety, and other scientific consulting, and adjacent environmental, engineering, and management-consulting codes absorb similar work [S1]. There is no public, code-specific lower-middle-market EBITDA-comp set in the consulted sources. Owner-age evidence and small-business transaction data are broad proxies, so both B and M carry wide ranges. A buyer should underwrite a specific subsegment, contract form, credential requirement, and client-transfer history rather than rely on this residual-code average.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — U.S. Census Bureau
sources.what_it_supports: Official NAICS hierarchy locating 54169/541690 within professional, scientific and technical services; used to frame the code as an official residual classification rather than a single specialty market.

sources.title: Large Firms With at Least 20 Employees Biggest AI Users — U.S. Census Bureau
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS reports overall business AI use of 17%–20% between December 2025 and May 2026, notes less than 20% use among firms with four or fewer employees, and describes AI-business-function measurement.

sources.title: BizBuySell Insight Report — Data Tables
sources.what_it_supports: Public small-business listings and 2025 reported transaction tables with revenue and cash-flow multiples; used only as a micro/SDE market anchor, not as a 541690 EBITDA comp.

sources.title: State of Owner Readiness — Exit Planning Institute
sources.what_it_supports: National and regional owner-readiness survey program; its published summary states that 51% of the current American business market is owned by Baby Boomers and only 20%–30% of businesses brought to market sell.

sources.title: Specialty Consulting M&A Report — 1H 2025 — Equiteq
sources.what_it_supports: Specialty consulting deal activity, buyer interest in high-quality sector expertise, analytical/AI capability, proprietary IP and scalable delivery; explicitly a global specialty-consulting proxy rather than a 541690 comp set.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Task-based estimate for a heterogeneous specialist-advisory mix: drafting, analysis, workflow and back office are exposed, while accountable expertise, client trust and physical/field work remain. The supplied 4-digit mix is a disclosed proxy.
caveats: 541690 is a catch-all and the supplied 4-digit 5416 role mix overstates management analysts relative to several technical niches.
The RESIDUAL includes both highly analytical specialists and field-based work; 0.35 is a bounded mixed-task judgment, not an observed fraction.

role_judgments.rationale: Analysis, research synthesis and presentation drafting are exposed; client diagnosis and accountable advice persist.
Planning and internal reporting automate, while owner relationships and delivery accountability remain.
Research, synthesis and routine insight production are AI-exposed, with client interpretation retained.
Proposal preparation automates, but referral and expert selling remain human.
Tracking, reporting and scheduling automate; cross-party coordination persists.
Routine inquiry handling and status updates are exposed, with complex service recovery retained.
Documentation and process work automate while specialized operations judgment persists.
Code, data and modeling workflow are AI-augmented, not fully removed.
Routine bookkeeping, reconciliation and document preparation are highly exposed.
Administrative HR workflow and drafting are highly exposed, with sensitive decisions retained.
Residual combines technical desk specialists with field, safety and scientific advisory work; the physical share limits replacement.

pi_dist.rationale: Project/hourly advisory permits clients to rescope hours as AI reduces production time; recurring compliance, seasonal and shared-savings engagements retain more savings.
caveats: No public 541690 revenue-by-fee-form mix was found; this is a dominant-subsegment estimate.

pi_moat.rationale: Specialist credentials, liability, local data and a delivery system can protect a firm, but named-expert relationships and broad code heterogeneity lower transferability.
caveats: This measures firm retention of savings, not the separate terminal-demand floor.

t50_years.rationale: Broad business AI use is still below 50%; five years reflects fragmented small firms and validation/physical-work limits after tools are already available.
caveats: BTOS is all-business, all-function evidence, not a 541690 effective-adoption measure.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Uses the top of Census’s 17%–20% broad business-use band as a conservative proxy for current use in this digital-service-heavy but small-firm code.
caveats: This is usage in any business function, not task-level adoption or a code-specific survey.

historical_analogs.value: Cloud collaboration, CRM and analytical-software adoption in fragmented professional services suggests tools spread before process redesign; AI’s output validation and client-confidentiality burden makes effective replacement slower than simple tool access.
historical_analogs.rationale: Qualitative adoption analogy used only to frame the timing estimate.
caveats: No verified 541690 historical analogue series was identified.

selection.rationale: Uses EPI’s broad statement that 51% of the American business market is owned by Baby Boomers as a directional small-business ownership proxy.
caveats: Not a 541690 or owners-over-60 census; Baby Boomer status is an imperfect age proxy.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: EPI reports that only 20%–30% of businesses that go to market actually sell, documenting a broad owner-transition shortfall.
caveats: Broad owner-readiness evidence, not a code-specific successor count.

active_consolidators.rationale: A conservative nonzero count represents observable specialty-consulting buyer appetite, not a claim that three named firms buy all 541690 niches.
caveats: Equiteq is global specialty consulting and does not enumerate code-specific active consolidators on the public page.

buy_mult.rationale: Lower-middle-market EBITDA buy anchor between micro/SDE marketplace evidence and specialist-consulting strategic interest, discounted for key-person and fee-form risk.
caveats: BizBuySell reports small-business cash-flow/SDE metrics, not 541690 EBITDA; Equiteq is global specialty consulting and does not publish a 541690 EBITDA comp on its public page.

exit_mult.rationale: Assumes a modest premium only if a buyer builds transferable client relationships, repeatable delivery and second-line expertise.
caveats: This is not an observed exit multiple; a key-person practice may exit below its entry multiple.

pricing_structure.value: Predominantly project and hourly expert fees, with niche recurring compliance, seasonal-retainer and shared-savings arrangements.
pricing_structure.rationale: Residual-code and specialty-consulting evidence support a mixed advisory model; the stated fee-form mix is a disclosed operating hypothesis.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Hourly/project heavy to recurring/shared-savings heavy by niche
caveats: No public 541690 revenue split by pricing model was located.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: A high task-exposure estimate coexists with a five-year adoption path because effective deployment still requires workflow redesign, review and client acceptance; neither consolidation nor owner age is used for A.

terminal_value.justification: AI can increasingly supply generic analysis and reduce billable desk work, while regulated/accountable expertise and field observation retain paid demand in selected niches.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No; the 4-digit occupation mix, broad BTOS adoption, broad owner evidence, micro/SDE market data and global specialty-consulting M&A evidence are disclosed proxies.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Hourly and project pricing exposes AI-produced hours to rescoping and competitive bids; recurring compliance, seasonal and shared-savings arrangements can retain more savings.

dataset_mismatch.description: The deterministic role mix is 4-digit 5416 while 541690 is a heterogeneous residual code; labor share is retained without modification.
sensitivity: Lower field/physical-work share would raise V.
A more owner-expert-heavy target would lower transferable value capture and M.

subsegments: agricultural, safety and other scientific field consulting
market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Specialist technical advisory with a material analytical/documentation component
market_boundary.selection_basis: Best investment hypothesis from the official residual classification and specialty-consulting M&A evidence; no public 541690 revenue split identified.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A would range from 3.33 to 1.25 rather than 2.00.
A 0.5x estimated expansion may disappear or reverse for a key-person practice.
V is sensitive to the unlisted residual’s field versus desk-work mix.

reviewer_note: All score-driving sources were reopened on the access date. Public evidence supports broad adoption, classification and market context; the code-specific pricing and EBITDA inputs are candid estimates with disclosed proxy limits.
